item.title: This is absolutely Amazing and Reading this was an Absolute JOY
item.content:encoded:
Ok but the title explains all but im going to explain how much I love this article in more detail anyway.

Going through the "Underrated" / "Underread" section of the SCP Wiki section, I immediately opened the article with the least likes and I was not disappointed. The concept of this seemingly unassuming shawl, so delicate and not so white that it seems "too" innocent or too unassuming. It looks like something I would wear everyday!

But plot twist! It actually has the ability to make things flammable and to perhaps set things on fire! That within itself isn't very strong, but there's this other element to it which ties to cults, and how its anomalous properties are only triggered when the wearer is a 7+ women…

The article explores themes of womanhood and femininity and the, as Simone de Beauvoir puts it (I paraphrase) "The Stylistics of the historical idea of a woman" and how it relates to society, religion, and cults. Maybe I'm looking too much into this article, maybe this isn't what you intended to convey with this piece of writing, and maybe you didn't expect a reference to political philosophies in the comment section of your writing.

But this was my relationship to the article in terms of what it means to me, and I loved it.

item.title: Re: Image Template
item.content:encoded: I… don't really know what happened there. It was definitely not my intention to use tables. But thanks for the better html!

item.title: Image Template
item.content:encoded:
[[include component:image-block
name=THE NAME OF YOUR IMAGE FILE|
caption=THE TEXT YOU WANT SHOWN UNDERNEATH YOUR IMAGE
]]

Just upload the file into the article, then set the name to match the one on the file. It looks 10x better than using tables. Hope this helps!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To add onto this, Roseafaer has close links with the Serpent's Hand1, as the Hand considers Roseafaer to be one of the last vestiges of anomalous technology that isn't under the control of something like the GOC or Foundation, and will defend it with their lives.

The Foundation doesn't want to force the hand anymore then is needed.

Footnotes
1. Will be expanded in future pieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First of all, they are supervising the entire time. And secondly… self-abnegation is involved in multiple religious traditions and is undertaken voluntarily in the hopes of a spiritual reward on investment, so the Lucias are (at least as reported) getting a net improvement in quality of life by being allowed to participate in their ritual (at the risk of burned fingers) rather than being forbidden from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon reading this SCP, it personally seems weird to me why the Foundation would willingly relinquish custody of an active SCP to a civilian population which the article admits appear to not be fully capable of controlling the object's effects (the burns on the subject).

Perhaps this requires more explanation.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Just another small contribution to the Roseafaer mythos.

Much credit to Amelia Wright for creating Roseafaer and cybersqyd for reviewing.

Image sources are here:

+ show block
– hide block
